title: Quadrella torulosa
taxonomy: Quadrella torulosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Capparaceae. The plant is native to tropical Africa and is also found in parts of Asia. Quadrella torulosa is a climbing or twining plant that grows up to 10 meters in height. It has heart-shaped leaves and produces clusters of small, white flowers. The plant's classification is further supported by its inclusion in the Capparaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the caper family.

intro: Quadrella torulosa, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy, is a perennial plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Quadrella torulosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
